english: Comp. ši-ki-ma (shi ki ma), ˳či-ka-ma ('chi ka ma) 1. sbst. dying, death, ši-ki-ma-ru (shi ki ma ru) in dying. — 2. adj. dying, ši-ki-ma yod (shi ki ma yod) (or ˳či-ka-ma yod ('chi ka ma yod)) he is at the point of death, he is at death's door. — *ši-kan* (*shi mkhan*) col. the deceased, the dead. — ši-sno (shi bsngo) Sch.: 'blessing for one deceased'. — ši-čos (shi chos) religious ceremonies for the dead Sch. — ši-ša (shi sha) flesh of animals that have died of themselves, the only flesh which a strict Buddhist is allowed to eat, and which accordingly in Buddhist countries is frequently consumed.
W. a sort of early barley.
Ssk. for krims (khrims), tsúl-krims (tshul khrims) custom, manner, moral law.

1. for čig (cig) (q.v.) after a final s. — 2. louse, mi-šig (mi shig) common louse, lúg-šig (lug shig) sheep-louse, tick, kyí-šig (khyi shig) flea, (lha)-˳dre-šig ((lha) 'dre shig) bug; *ḍag (lit. brag)-šig-pa* (*brag shig pa*) W. mite, wood-louse, tick; šig ˳t'ú-ba (shig 'thu ba) B., *ltá-če (lta ce), rúg-če* (*rug ce*) W. to look for lice, to louse, šig bsál-ba (shig bsal ba) to clean from lice; šig-čan (shig can) Sch. also šig-po (shig po) or šig-šig-po (shig shig po) infested with lice, lousy; šig-nád (shig nad) pedicular disease; šig-sró (shig sro) lice and nits S.g.

1. standing or lying close together, close-banded Mil. nt., C. cf. ɣšig-pa (gshig pa), ɣšib-pa (gshib pa). — 2. trembling, tottering, wavering; with mig (mig): looking this way and that, looking about, perh. also: roiling (the eyes).
rocking, as trees moved by the wind Mil.; šigs-šigs yom-yóm (shigs shigs yom yom) waving, moving to and fro, shaken etc., also fig. Pth.

I. gerundial particle for čiṅ (cing) after a final s. II. sbst. 1. tree, bzá-šiṅ (bza' shing) fruit-tree, rtsi-šiṅ (rtsi shing) v. rtsi (rtsi); ljóṅ-šiṅ (ljong shing) a beautiful green leafy tree, skám-šiṅ (skam shing) a dry withered tree. — 2. wood, šiṅ žig (shing zhig) some wood; kán-šiṅ (mkhan shing) timber, timber-wood, búd-šiṅ (bud shing) firewood, fuel, skám-šiṅ (skam shing) dry wood; yám-šiṅ (yam shing) Cs.: 'a small quantity of wood thrown into the fire for sacrifice'. — 3. a piece of wood, log, billet, *šiṅ ṅyi sum tob* (*shing nyi sum thob*) W. put two or three pieces (to the fire); stump, stub of a tree Glr.; *tú-pag-gi šiṅ* (*mthu pag gi shing*) W. gun-stock; sróg-šiṅ (srog shing) axle, axle-tree. Comp. šiṅ-kúr-ti (shing khur ti) a carrying-frame Lh. — šiṅ-kyu (shing kyu) a wooden hook. — šiṅ-rkaṅ (shing rkang) Schr. a wooden leg, a crutch. — šiṅ-rkéd (shing rked) the upper part of the trunk of a tree. — šiṅ-K'aṅ (shing khang) 1. a wooden house, log-house. 2. shed or outhouse for wood. — šiṅ-k'u (shing khu) sap, juice of trees. — šiṅ-k'ur (shing khur) a load of wood. — šiṅ-k'ri (shing khri) wooden chair. — šiṅ-mk'an (shing mkhan) worker in wood, carpenter, joiner. — šiṅ-rgón (shing rgon) Sch. wood pecker, šiṅ-rgon k'rá-bo (shing rgon khra bo) the spotted woodpecker, šiṅ-rgon mgo-nág (shing rgon mgo nag) black woodpecker. — šiṅ-rgyál (shing rgyal) a tree of extraordinary height or circumference, a giant-tree. — šiṅ-mṅár (shing mngar) licorice Sch., Wts.; a sort of cinnamon W. — šiṅ-čás (shing chas) 1. wooden utensils, implements. 2. tools for working wood Sch. — šiṅ-tog (shing tog) fruits of trees, fruit. — šiṅ-rta (shing rta) v. that article. — šiṅ-stan (shing stan) chopping-block Ld. — šiṅ-t'ags (shing thags) wooden enclosure. — šiṅ-t'ún (shing thun) wood-picker, gatherer of wind-fallen wood. — šiṅ-dúm (shing dum) log, billet, block. — šiṅ-dra (shing dra) wooden lattice-work; wooden paling C., W. — šiṅ-druṅ-pa (shing drung pa) one sitting under a tree, i.e. an ascetic, Burn. I, 309. — šiṅ-ɣdúgs (shing gdugs) the leafy crown of a tree Sch. — šiṅ-sdóṅ (shing sdong) trunk, stem of a tree; a tree; block. — šiṅ-p'ráṅ (shing phran) a small tree, a shrub, bush Sch. — šiṅ-bál (shing bal) cotton from the cotton-tree Cs., cf. šal-ma-li-šiṅ (shal ma li shing). — šiṅ-bu (shing bu) a small piece of wood, šiṅ-bu sor-bži-pa (shing bu sor bzhi pa) a piece of wood four inches broad or long Tar. — šiṅ-˳brás (shing 'bras) fruit. — šiṅ-smán (shing sman) medicine prepared from wood Sch. — šiṅ-rtsá (shing rtsa) root of a tree. — šiṅ-rtsi (shing rtsi) resin Cs. — šiṅ-rtsé (shing rtse) top of a tree. — šiṅ-ts'a (shing tsha) cinnamon (having a 'saltish' taste, as is expressly stated S.g.); *šiṅ-ts'a lób-ma* (*shing tsha lob ma*) W. bay-leaf, laurel-leaf. — šiṅ-ts'ál (shing tshal) chip, shaving, splinter. *šiṅ-ts'ógs* (*shing tshogs*) W. forest. — šiṅ-ɣžóṅ (shing gzong) a wooden basin, trough, tub. — *šiṅ-žóg* (*shing zhog*) (lit. bžogs (bzhogs)) chip, splint W.; shavings brought off by the plane C. — šiṅ-záṅ (shing zang) wood-rasp Sch. — *šiṅ-zél* (*shing gzel*) a small chip, a very small and thin piece of wood, a splinter, *šiṅ-zél zug soṅ* (*shing gzel zug song*) W. I have run a splinter into (my hand or foot).
